zh.Davenham, Cheshire, England: 英國英格蘭爾達文哈姆
zh.lifeSummary: Joan de Heath是John de Heath和Mrs. de Heath的女兒，於1432年6月1日出生在英國英格蘭爾達文哈姆。她於1475年6月1日與Hugh de Bostock*在英國英格蘭爾達文哈姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1495年6月1日在英國爾享年63歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Heath
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) heath’ (Middle English he(a)th, Old English hǣth) or else a habitational name from any of numerous places called with this word. The same word also denoted heather, the characteristic plant of heathland areas. This surname has also been established in Dublin, Ireland, since the late 16th century.

variants.name: Haith
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and East Yorkshire): variant of   Heath  .

variants.name: Heathman
meanings: English: topographic name denoting a dweller ‘on the heath’, from Middle English he(a)th + man(ne). Compare Heath and Heather .

variants.name: Hatton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Hatton (Cheshire, Derbyshire, Lincolnshire, Middlesex, Shropshire, Staffordshire, Warwickshire), Hatton Hall (Cheshire), or Cold and High Hatton (Shropshire). The placenames derive from Old English hǣth ‘heath, heather’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Heath .
English: variant of Atton with prosthetic H-, a topographic name from Middle English atte toun ‘(dweller) at the settlement’ (Old English æt thǣm tūn(e)). Compare Towne .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Chatáin, a patronymic from a personal name meaning ‘servant of (Saint) Catan’; see McHatton .

variants.name: Heather
meanings: English: topographic name denoting a ‘dweller on the heath’, an agent derivative of Middle English hethe; compare Heath . The name may also be habitational name from Heather (Leicestershire), from Old English hǣthor ‘heathland’ (hǣth ‘heath, heather’ + the noun suffix -or) or hǣddre ‘heather’. This surname is widespread in southern England, and also established in Ireland.

variants.name: Health
meanings: Altered form of English and Scottish   Heath  .
English and Scottish: perhaps a nickname from the vocabulary word health. — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is 105.

variants.name: Hoad
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, from Middle English hoth ‘heath’ (Old English hāth, a byform of Old English hǣth), or a habitational name from a place so named, such as Hoath in Kent. This form was restricted in the Middle Ages to southeastern England, and the surname is still largely confined to Kent, Surrey, and Sussex. Compare Heath .

variants.name: Heid
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Eckhart, Gerd, Gotthilf, Heinz, Klaus, Konrad, Kurt, Manfred, Ralf, Uli.
German, Swiss German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name from Middle High German heide, German Heide ‘heath, moor, open, uncultivated land’. Compare Heath .
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Haido, a short form of any of various compound names beginning with haid ‘heath’ (i.e. wasteland, often with heather).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Joan de Heath was born on 1 June 1432, in Davenham, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of John de Heath and Mrs. de Heath. She married Hugh de Bostock* on 1 June 1475, in Davenham, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 1 June 1495, in Cheshire, England, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Joan de Heath est née le 1 juin 1432, à Davenham, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fille de John de Heath et Mrs. de Heath. Elle a épousé Hugh de Bostock* le 1 juin 1475, à Davenham, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 1 juin 1495, à Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 63 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Joan de Heath wurde am 1. Juni 1432 in Davenham, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, als Tochter von John de Heath und Mrs. de Heath geboren. Sie hat am 1. Juni 1475 in Davenham, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Hugh de Bostock* geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 1. Juni 1495 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Joan de Heath è nata il il 1 giugno 1432 nel Davenham, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlia di John de Heath e Mrs. de Heath. Lei sposato Hugh de Bostock* il 1 giugno 1475, nel Davenham, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 1 giugno 1495, nel Cheshire, England, all'età di 63 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Heath(은)는 1432년 6월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 대븐함에서 John de Heath(와)과 Mrs. de Heath의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1475년 6월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 대븐함에서 Hugh de Bostock*(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1495년 6월 1일 영국 체실르에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan de Heath родилась 1 июня 1432 г. в местности давенам, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. Её родителей звали John de Heath и Mrs. de Heath. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh de Bostock* 1 июня 1475 г. в местности давенам, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 1 июня 1495 г. в местности чешир, Англия в возрасте 63 лет.
